卓詠欣，藝術行政、內容策展、品牌編輯。I work between festivals, museums, publications and independent presses — shaping how culture gets written about, organised, and shared.

01 — About

Not a designer.
A connector of ideas.

「用內容、設計思考與文化策展，把不同的人連結起來。」

My work sits at the intersection of arts administration, editorial writing and brand content — coordinating festivals across Hong Kong, Taiwan and beyond, editing magazines and yearbooks, and translating culture into words people actually want to read.

I've spent the last several years moving between newsroom desks, museum education teams, independent publishers and cross-cultural theatre festivals — always asking the same question: how do we make this story land, for this audience, in this moment?

Outside of client work, I keep a running practice of my own: a camping journal turned small brand, a street photography project documenting Hong Kong's disappearing trades, and hand-bound zines made one fold at a time.

02 — Selected Work

Festivals, publications & brand projects.

A working index of editorial, curatorial and administrative projects across Hong Kong and Taiwan, 2023–2026.

香港 · Hong Kong2026.07

《諸神會藝術節》暨《榮念曾青年藝術學堂 2026》

節目統籌及行政 · Programme Coordination & Administration

Co-presented by 進念·二十面體 and 香港當代文化中心 to mark the 30th and 20th anniversaries of the two institutions — a cross-cultural theatre festival of seven directors, seven performances, spanning installation, contemporary dance, opera, sound and ritual. I helped coordinate programme content and execution across seven performances, forums and workshops; edited bilingual programme text, website content, invitations and ticketing pages; and liaised with artists travelling from Tokyo, Seoul, Phnom Penh, Singapore and Chongqing.

FestivalEditorialCoordination
香港 · Hong Kong2025.08

印藝新浪潮：#印500+

策劃與主編 · Editor-in-Chief

A publication reframing print as a creative and cultural medium beyond paper — marking the 500th issue of the Graphic Arts Association of Hong Kong's monthly journal. Awarded Bronze, Book & Periodical Printing (Softcover), 36th Hong Kong Print Awards.

PublishingEditorialAward
香港 · Hong Kong2025.08

The Education University of Hong Kong

Website Content Writer & Translator

Translated the PTSITC (Principals and Teachers Synergistic Innovation Training Centre) website and product content into Traditional Chinese (zh-HK), with a full UX copy recommendation report to make the interface language clearer and more natural for Chinese-reading users.

TranslationUX Writing
台灣 · Taiwan2024.11

咖哩飯出版社 · 《與山的N種方式》

策展與出版 · Curation & Publishing

"六感" — a zine exploring Yangmingshan through the six senses: sight, sound, touch, smell, taste and beyond. Designed around the elevation of 擎天崗 (770m), with a mountain-scent vial and psychology quiz stickers tucked inside, so reading becomes its own small hike.

ZineCurationIndependent Press
台灣 · Taiwan2024.01

石碇螢火蟲復藝節

策展 · Exhibition Curation

"以家為守台" — beyond guided walks, the festival built an aesthetic communal table, a local-regeneration forum and workshops rooted in local culture, bringing together residents and students from National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University.

FestivalCommunityWorkshop
台灣 · Taiwan2023.09 – 2025.09

Everyday Object

企劃編輯 · Editorial Planning & Interviews

Founded in 2013, Everyday Object is a lifestyle brand spanning online media, curated goods and its own print annual (since 2018). I planned, interviewed for and edited yearbook features and design/art profiles — from a light-filled Wenzhou Street apartment renovation to a Danish coffee pop-up with April Coffee — plus ongoing web and social content.

EditorialInterviewsBrand Content
台灣 · Taiwan2023.02 – 04

忠泰美術館 JUT Art Museum

推廣教育組 · 【字裡紙說】社群貼文企劃

A social content series for the museum's five-year anniversary, pairing architects, curators and writers with a single question: "紙是生活中俯拾皆是的材料，你曾否將腦海裡靈光乍現的意念躍然紙上？"

Public ProgrammeSocial Content
香港 · Hong Kong2022

M+ 博物館

數位媒體助理編輯 · Digital Assistant Editor

Assisted the digital content team of Hong Kong's new museum for visual culture ahead of and following its opening — supporting exhibition copy, M+ Stories features and website content across the museum's launch season.

MuseumDigital Editorial

05 — Personal Practice

Journals, zines & a life outdoors.

Independent projects run alongside client work.

昨日露營 · YESTERDAY CAMPING

明日，是今天是下一天，縱使今天是如何，還可以懷惴明天是一件曼妙之事。

昨日露營 Yesterday Camping began in 2021 as a personal blog piecing together the landscape of camping, one photo and one paragraph at a time. In 2022 it grew into 明日露營 Tomorrow Camping, a small goods label made with a local illustrator, first shown at Hong Kong's own craft-and-culture market that July.

漫遊我城 — a street photography series documenting trades and craftspeople disappearing from Hong Kong's streets: tailors, opera dressers, porcelain painters, barbers, basket weavers.

My First Zine — 《天黑前，要一起去散步嗎？》 A hand-folded zine on lojong, walking meditation, and the discipline of noticing — built around a Polaroid of dusk over Tamsui.
